touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Elena Reddy at PivotChannel introducing CRM automation capabilities. No response received.
Follow-up cold email to Elena highlighting Professional Services industry use cases. No response.
Third email attempt sharing case study on CRM workflow automation for mid-market firms. No response.
Cold call attempt to Elena Reddy. Left voicemail introducing value proposition around reducing manual CRM tasks.
Second cold call attempt. No answer, left brief follow-up voicemail.
LinkedIn message sent referencing Elena's recent role transition to Director of CS and how automation could help scale her team's efforts. No response.
Email sent with specific ROI calculator for 360-employee Professional Services firms. No response.
Follow-up email received response from Elena expressing initial interest in learning more about CRM automation for her CS team.

questions_asked.question: What exactly does your unlimited automation tier include?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited workflows, AI-powered data entry, custom triggers, and integration with all major CRM platforms.

touches.ai_summary: Additional cold email sent before qualification call scheduled. No response needed.
Cold call attempt during prospecting. Voicemail left.
Pre-qualification email confirming meeting time and sharing agenda. Elena confirmed attendance.

14-minute qualification call with Elena. Discussed current CRM pain points around manual data entry and lack of automation. Elena mentioned being new to the Director role (3 months) and looking to make early impact. Confirmed budget authority sits with procurement team.

questions_asked.question: What are your biggest CRM workflow challenges right now?
questions_asked.answer: Manual data entry takes up about 30% of our CS team's time, and we have no real automation in place.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: I'd need to bring in Omar and James from our Implementation team, and eventually procurement for any contracts.

next_steps: Send calendar invite with Implementation team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email after qualification call with discovery call agenda and request for Elena to invite Implementation team members.
Cold email template sent during prospecting phase. No response required.

37-minute discovery call with Elena exploring detailed use cases for CS team automation. Discussed current tech stack, integration requirements, and timeline. Elena mentioned wanting to move quickly to show results in her new role. Team size of 21 CS seats confirmed.

questions_asked.question: What does your current CRM tech stack look like?
questions_asked.answer: We're on Salesforce with some basic integrations, but nothing sophisticated for automation.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the timeline on this project?
questions_asked.answer: I want to have something in place within the quarter to demonstrate value in my first 6 months here.

questions_asked.question: How long does typical implementation take for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 4-6 weeks from contract signature to full deployment for 20-25 seats.

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation scheduled but rescheduled by Elena due to conflicting Implementation team meeting.

39-minute technical evaluation with Elena and Implementation team. Walked through automation workflows, integration capabilities, and unlimited automation tier features. Omar from Implementation raised questions about API limits and customization options.

questions_asked.question: Are there any API rate limits we should be aware of with unlimited automation?
questions_asked.answer: No hard limits on the unlimited tier, though we have fair-use policies to prevent abuse. Your use case would be well within normal parameters.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the automation workflows for our specific CS processes?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully customizable with our workflow builder and custom code options if needed.

questions_asked.question: What's your evaluation criteria for choosing an automation platform?
questions_asked.answer: Ease of use, integration depth with Salesforce, and ability to scale as we grow the CS team.

objections: Need to ensure this doesn't create additional complexity for the team to manage
next_steps: Provide custom demo for CS workflows

touches.ai_summary: 37-minute follow-up technical evaluation focusing on specific CS automation scenarios. Demonstrated custom workflows for their use cases. Team seemed engaged but Omar mentioned needing to evaluate ROI carefully before moving forward.

questions_asked.question: What kind of time savings do similar Professional Services firms typically see?
questions_asked.answer: Most see 40-60% reduction in manual CRM tasks within the first quarter, which for your team would be roughly 250-300 hours per month.

questions_asked.question: Do you have budget allocated for this type of solution this quarter?
questions_asked.answer: We have budget, but it would need to go through our standard procurement review process with Omar and James.

questions_asked.question: What does pricing look like for 21 seats with unlimited automation?
questions_asked.answer: I'll work up a formal proposal, but typically for your size and tier we're in the $20-25K ACV range.

objections: We need to make sure the ROI justifies the investment given our current budget constraints

touches.ai_summary: 7-minute proposal call with Elena to review pricing for 21 seats at unlimited automation tier. Presented $22,800 ACV proposal. Elena mentioned that procurement would need to review and that the pricing was higher than initially expected.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on this pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with my manager what we can do. I understand you're working within budget constraints.

touches.ai_summary: 19-minute negotiation call with Elena. Discussed revised pricing at $18,100 ACV (20.7% discount) to fit within approved budget. Elena appreciated the flexibility but mentioned she'd need to get Omar and James to sign off before moving forward. No executive sponsor mentioned.

questions_asked.question: Who on the executive team should we bring into the conversation at this stage?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, I think we can move forward with procurement approval. I have authority for CS tools within this budget range.

questions_asked.question: What's included in onboarding and support at this price point?
questions_asked.answer: Full implementation support, dedicated CSM, and unlimited training sessions for your team during the first 90 days.

objections: I need to make sure procurement is comfortable with the contract terms before we commit

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sent during prospecting. Response received acknowledging connection.
Executive meeting scheduled with Elena to discuss strategic value and final approval. Meeting was rescheduled due to Elena's scheduling conflict.
Follow-up email after rescheduled meeting confirming new time and agenda. Elena confirmed attendance and mentioned bringing final questions from procurement team.
next_steps: Prepare for executive meeting with procurement questions

touches.ai_summary: 31-minute executive meeting with Elena. Discussed implementation timeline, contract terms, and ROI projections. Elena seemed positive but mentioned procurement (Omar and James) had concerns about payment terms and wanted annual prepayment adjusted.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual prepayment?
questions_asked.answer: Our standard terms are annual, but let me check if we can accommodate quarterly billing given the relationship.

questions_asked.question: What's the timeline for getting procurement sign-off?
questions_asked.answer: If we can work out the payment terms, I think we could have this finalized within a week.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we want to add more seats mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: We can do mid-year seat additions pro-rated to your renewal date at the same per-seat rate.

objections: Procurement is concerned about annual prepayment for a new vendor relationship

touches.ai_summary: 19-minute executive meeting finalizing contract details. Confirmed quarterly payment terms approved. Elena and procurement team ready to move forward pending final contract review. No executive sponsor engaged, decision being made at Director/Implementation level.

questions_asked.question: Is there anyone else on the executive team we should loop in before finalizing?
questions_asked.answer: No, I have authority to approve this. Let's get the paperwork moving.

questions_asked.question: When can we start implementation once the contract is signed?
questions_asked.answer: We can kick off within 3-5 business days of signature with your dedicated implementation team.

next_steps: Send final contract with quarterly terms

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message exchange with Elena confirming contract sent for review. Elena responded positively, mentioning procurement review in progress.
